what_it_does: Defines an inline CSS recipe for v2 mockups, including shared color tokens, blueprint overlays, a footer band, title and heading treatments, accent roles, surface styles, a watermark, and an optional headless Chrome render check.
observations: The file explicitly says snippets should be copied inline into each mockup's v2.html and not imported or linked.
It defines root-level color tokens for page, warm and cool plates, nested surfaces, gold wash, accent colors, body text, and lines.
It specifies two low-opacity blueprint grid underlays positioned in the top corners of each frame.
It defines a bottom grounding band about 100px tall and instructs layouts to reserve about 110px for it.
It replaces uppercase letterspaced headings with title-case heading styles using charcoal, ochre, and bronze roles.
It states that gold should be used for figures or marks and never as a plate, zone, or background fill.
It includes plate, cool plate, and nested surface treatments with subtle shadows and warm-neutral backgrounds.
It defines a circular V watermark positioned above the footer band.
It includes an optional headless Chrome screenshot command that writes only to /tmp.
standouts: Self-contained copy-paste recipe rather than a shared token import.
Strong semantic assignment of color by visual role.
Explicit bottom-band layout reservation and watermark placement.
Optional render command with constraints about output location and screenshot interpretation.
conventions: Uses CSS custom properties for visual tokens.
Uses class-based snippets for reusable visual elements such as .bp, .gband, .title, .plate, .nested, and .mark.
Uses direct prose rules alongside CSS snippets.
Requires title case for titles and headings instead of uppercase letterspaced treatment.
Requires warm-neutral surfaces and avoids blue-tinted cool plates.
Prohibits gold fills while allowing gold marks and figures.
concerns: The instruction to copy snippets inline into multiple mockups creates a manual synchronization surface inside the file's stated workflow.
The render check depends on a specific Chrome command and viewport assumptions that are only partially described here.
Several placement instructions require per-layout adaptation, but the file does not define constraints for resolving collisions with existing content.
notes: Brief someone on this file as the v2 visual recipe that makes mockups share warm-neutral tokens, subtle structure, restrained accents, footer grounding, and self-contained CSS.
questions: Which exact mockup layouts receive which adapted positions for the blueprint underlays and footer payloads?
What content should be used inside each footer grounding band?
When existing mockup content conflicts with the reserved footer space or watermark, which local layout rule should decide the adjustment?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it provides inline snippets and rules for applying shared v2 visual treatments without building a shared token system.
intention_signals: It seems to be trying to make many mockups visually consistent through a portable recipe while keeping implementation local to each v2.html file.
novelty: A design-system-like recipe expressed as copyable inline CSS plus prose constraints, including visual role rules and a local screenshot check.
contribution: Contributes a compact ledger of visual tokens, reusable CSS fragments, role-based accent rules, surface treatments, frame overlays, footer grounding, watermark placement, and render-check instructions that a fused system would preserve as design substrate material.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a self-contained v2 mockup recipe containing CSS tokens, reusable visual classes, color-role rules, layout treatments, and a headless Chrome render check.
intention_for_embedding: It was for applying the same warm-neutral DNA v2 visual language across multiple mockups without introducing a shared imported token system.
